description: In 2003, religious leaders in northern Nigeria boycotted the polio vaccination campaign, alleging it was a Western conspiracy to cause infertility. This sparked a regional health crisis that set back global eradication efforts for years.

key_events.description: Supreme Council for Sharia in Nigeria issues formal warning against polio vaccine.
Kano state governor suspends all polio vaccination activities.
WHO reports new cases of polio linked to the suspension of the vaccination program.
National vaccination campaigns resume with intervention from religious leaders.

calibration_notes: Useful for testing how misinformation impacts public sentiment regarding scientific interventions in sensitive cultural contexts.
